sub_spec:
## Scope

Harden the inner loop scripts (`loop.sh` / `loop.ps1`) with the full set of robustness features specified in SPEC.md:

- **Phase retry logic** (Retry-Same-Phase): failed iterations retry the same phase with the next round-robin provider; `cyclePosition` only advances on success
- **Phase prerequisites**: `build` requires unchecked TODO.md tasks; `review` requires commits since last plan â€” missing prerequisites force fallback phases
- **`MAX_PHASE_RETRIES` safety valve**: after all providers fail the same phase N times, advance anyway and log `phase_retry_exhausted`
- **CLAUDECODE environment sanitization**: unset at script top and before each provider call in both `loop.sh` and `loop.ps1`
- **Provider stderr capture**: separate stdout/stderr capture in `Invoke-Provider`/`invoke_provider` for failure classification
- **Session lockfile**: `session.lock` PID file â€” refuse start if alive PID detected; clean up in `finally`/`trap`
- **Child PID tracking**: track spawned provider PIDs; kill all on loop exit
- **Per-iteration timeout**: configurable with precedence: prompt frontmatter `timeout` â†’ `ALOOP_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` â†’ built-in default (identical in both scripts)
- **`run_id` in log entries**: unique run ID per session start in all `log.jsonl` entries
- **Cross-platform parity**: all behaviors identical in `loop.sh` and `loop.ps1`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Failed iterations do not advance `cyclePosition`; next iteration retries same phase with next provider
- [ ] `build` phase forces `plan` when no unchecked TODO.md tasks exist; logged as `phase_prerequisite_miss`
- [ ] `review` phase forces `build` when no commits since last plan; logged as `phase_prerequisite_miss`
- [ ] After `MAX_PHASE_RETRIES` consecutive failures on same phase, advance with `phase_retry_exhausted` log
- [ ] `CLAUDECODE` is unset at top of `loop.sh` and `loop.ps1`
- [ ] `CLAUDECODE` is unset before each provider call in both scripts (defense-in-depth)
- [ ] `CLAUDECODE` deleted from `process.env` at `aloop/cli/src/index.ts` entry
- [ ] Provider stderr is captured separately and included in failure log entries
- [ ] `session.lock` PID file written on start; checked for alive PID before start; cleaned up in `finally`/`trap`
- [ ] All spawned child PIDs tracked and killed on loop exit
- [ ] Per-iteration timeout respects frontmatter `timeout` > `ALOOP_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` > default
- [ ] Default timeout value is identical in `loop.sh` and `loop.ps1`
- [ ] Every `log.jsonl` entry includes a `run_id` field unique to the session run
- [ ] Both loop scripts pass their existing test suites with these additions

## Architectural Context

This epic touches only the inner loop scripts (`aloop/bin/loop.sh`, `aloop/bin/loop.ps1`) and the CLI entry point (`aloop/cli/src/index.ts`). Per CONSTITUTION rule 1: loop scripts are dumb runners â€” no business logic, no GH calls. Per rule 2: all host-side operations stay in the runtime. Per rule 3: the loop never exits mid-cycle. These changes are purely mechanical loop hardening within those boundaries. `loop-plan.json` fields: `cyclePosition`, `iteration`, `allTasksMarkedDone`, `finalizerPosition`, `finalizer[]` are already partially defined â€” this epic ensures they are correctly maintained.

## Dependencies

None â€” this is the foundation epic.
